ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002539 covers salad food products broadly; no specific code exists for a four-topping customisable salad variant. Exact match used for the general category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GG covers salads as a category. No specific facet code for a four-topping variant exists; exact match for the general category used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 250 g (one bowl or plate), representing a standard restaurant or canteen portion of mixed salad with four toppings and dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Salad (4 toppings) — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Salad (4 toppings), food code F08010043. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed vegetable salad with dressing (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a four-topping customisable salad. GI of 32 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed vegetable salad with dressing (GI ~32, low category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (32) × available carbohydrate per serving (250 g × 4.4 g/100 g = 11 g) / 100 = 3.5. This is a defensible analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for precision.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg per 100 g; dressing and topping choices can substantially increase sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dressing on the side and avoid high-sodium toppings such as processed meats, pickled vegetables, or aged cheeses, as these can push the total sodium content well above 400 mg per 100 g and undermine blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4.4 g per 100 g), low sugar (3.75 g per 100 g), and dietary fibre (1.84 g per 100 g) yield a low glycaemic load of approximately 3.5 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: This salad is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when dressed with low-sugar vinaigrette and topped with non-starchy vegetables or lean protein; patients should avoid sweet dressings, croutons, and dried fruit toppings that can significantly raise the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.65 g per 100 g), high vegetable content providing dietary fibre (1.84 g per 100 g) and micronutrients supportive of cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The vegetable-based profile with low saturated fat and meaningful dietary fibre supports cardiovascular health; however, patients should avoid creamy or high-saturated-fat dressings and processed meat toppings that increase saturated fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidant-rich vegetable base with low saturated fat supports anti-inflammatory dietary patterns
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vegetable base provides antioxidants and fibre consistent with anti-inflammatory dietary principles; patients with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should avoid tomato and bell pepper toppings, and should select olive-oil-based dressings over creamy alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salad with Four Toppings (HPB food code F08010043) is a customisable mixed-vegetable salad dish consisting of a base of fresh mixed greens and raw vegetables combined with a choice of four toppings and a salad dressing. It is a minimally processed, assembled dish common in Western-style canteens, cafeterias, and health-food outlets in Singapore and internationally. The base typically includes lettuce, cucumber, tomato, and carrot, while toppings may include items such as hard-boiled egg, grilled chicken, tuna, cheese, croutons, seeds, or additional vegetables. The dressing is typically a vinaigrette or creamy dressing served on the side or mixed in. The texture is crisp and fresh, with flavour varying significantly depending on topping and dressing selection. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 64 kcal, 1.94 g protein, 4.23 g fat, 4.4 g carbohydrate, 3.75 g sugar, 1.84 g dietary fibre, and 231 mg sodium. The low carbohydrate and low glycaemic index (estimated GI ~32) make it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease. The sodium content at 231 mg per 100 g is borderline for hypertension management and is highly sensitive to dressing and topping choices. The dish is classified as vegetarian in its base form but may include animal-derived toppings. It is suitable for low-calorie, plant-based, and low-glycaemic dietary patterns.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (food code F08010043) at confidence 0.95. This is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this specific variant; this is documented transparently.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at 0.65 g per 100 g based on typical fat composition of mixed vegetable salad with dressing (approximately 15% of total fat as saturated), as the HPB source does not report saturated fat breakdown separately for this entry; this is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. Fields set to -1 (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g): detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food code. These fields are set to -1 per Veritas numeric hygiene rules. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed vegetable salad with dressing; direct GI measurement for this specific four-topping variant is not available in any approved source. Dietary tags include keto as inherited from input; this is defensible given the low carbohydrate content (4.4 g per 100 g) but may vary depending on topping selection. Vegan flag set to false as toppings may include animal-derived products; base salad without toppings would be vegan. This is a Western-cuisine generic dish; no mandatory local-language names apply under Veritas multilingual rules.
